zh.Latham, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾拉堂姆 聖 詹姆斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Elena de Latham於1274年出生在英國藍卡爾拉堂姆 聖 詹姆斯時，她的父親Thomas De Latham30歲，母親Samantha Maryanne Reeves20歲。她和Sir Robert Dalton至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世在英國藍卡爾比斯法姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Latham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Lathom in Lancashire, or occasionally perhaps from Laytham in Yorkshire. The placenames mean ‘at the barns’, from hlǫthum, the dative plural form of Old Norse hlatha ‘barn, store house’.

variants.name: Laden
meanings: German: from a shortened form of Ladengast ‘invite the guest’, a nickname for an innkeeper.
German: from von der Laden, a nickname for a joiner or for the keeper of a shrine or the chest of a guild, from Middle Low German lade ‘chest’.

variants.name: Lathan
meanings: English (mainly northeastern counties): variant of   Latham  .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Elena
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Helen  , now used quite frequently in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Elena de Latham was born in 1274, in Lathom St James, Lancashire, England, her father, Thomas De Latham, was 30 and her mother, Samantha Maryanne Reeves, was 20. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Robert Dalton. She died in Bispham, Lancashire, England.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Robert Dalton. Elle est décédée à Bispham, Lancashire, Angleterre.

ko.Kingston-Upton-Hull, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 킨그스턴 업 훌 스트 메리
ko.lifeSummary: Mary Elena de Latham(이)가 1274년 영국 랭카실르 래톰 스트 자메스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas De Latham(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Samantha Maryanne Reeves(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert Dalton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 랭카실르 비스프아에서 사망했다.
